search_queries_used: site:dha.gov.ae conjunctivitis contact lens wearer PDF
site:college-optometrists.org contact lens infiltrative keratitis guideline
site:college-optometrists.org microbial keratitis contact lens guideline
site:rcophth.ac.uk contact lens red eye guideline
candidate_sources_rejected: CLARE acronym summaries without exact guidance
rejection_reasons: Patient pages are not clinician guidance.
Unopened acronym summaries were not mapped.
population_applicability: Partial: exact for contact-lens red-eye and keratitis concerns; not exact for every intolerance, dry-eye, overwear syndrome, or lens-fitting problem.
setting_applicability: Direct for DHA telehealth triage and UK first-contact eye care; lens fit and corneal staining require in-person assessment.
UAE_applicability: DHA guidance is directly applicable in Dubai; College guidance requires UAE clinician review and local referral pathways.
recency_verification: DHA Issue 2, College CL-keratitis Version 9, and microbial-keratitis Version 14 were opened on 2026-07-12.
superseded_check: No newer official DHA or College versions were identified.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports mapped vision, severe-pain, photophobia, corneal, redness, and contact-lens warning fields.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Lists reduced visual acuity, ciliary flush, photophobia, severe foreign-body sensation, corneal opacity, fixed pupil, severe headache with nausea, post-traumatic red eye, and immune compromise.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports mapped contact-lens history, red eye, discharge, vision, corneal concern, lens fit or hygiene, and visual-acuity documentation.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Requires history and visual review for red eye and discharge, distinguishes discharge character, asks about vision, foreign-body sensation, photophobia, trauma and contact lenses, and highlights keratitis risk in contact-lens wearers.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports mapped contact-lens cessation, hygiene, response review, and follow-up documentation.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports clinician-led management, contact-lens hygiene or cessation discussion, response review, infection-control education, and referral for persistent symptoms, reduced acuity, photophobia, corneal opacity, painful red eye, severe headache with nausea, hyphema, trauma, or inability to open the eye; dose tables are not mapped.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports mapped contact-lens, unilateral redness, watering, foreign-body sensation, discomfort, photophobia, and corneal-opacity fields.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports contact-lens use, usually unilateral redness, watering, foreign-body sensation, discomfort, photophobia, peripheral corneal opacity, and the need to distinguish sight-threatening microbial keratitis.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports mapped lens hygiene, case care, overnight-wear, wearing-schedule, cessation, and reassessment documentation.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports clinician-documented temporary lens cessation, hand and lens-case hygiene, avoidance of overnight wear, wearing-schedule review, and reassessment context.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports mapped severe pain, blurred vision, discharge, and sight-threatening infection warning context.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports usually unilateral acute redness, moderate or severe pain, photophobia, watering, discharge, blurred vision, corneal opacity or ulcer, and contact-lens, injury, surgery, exposure, dry-eye, or immune-risk context.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports mapped urgent return and ophthalmology context without adding treatment.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Identifies microbial keratitis as sight-threatening and supports urgent ophthalmology documentation; treatment specifics are not mapped.

unresolved_source_gaps: CLARE, broad contact-lens intolerance, dry-eye contacts, trauma and chemical negatives, eyelid and pupil assessment, fluorescein, red reflex, fundus, fields, medication selection, and fixed review timing remain unsupported.
Keratitis or infection must not be inferred automatically.
Qualified clinician review has not been completed.
